---
title: "GO analysis Supp table"
author: "Elizabeth Campbell"
date: "2026-01-07"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# FIXED SCRIPT - Handles Different Column Name Formats
# This version automatically detects your column names

################################################################################
# SETUP
################################################################################

library(dplyr)

# Set working directory (UPDATE THIS to your folder)
setwd("C:/Users/Lizzy/OneDrive/Documents/UH_Manoa_Classes/IWG_Chapter_2/Analysis_Outputs_and_Visualizations/Gene Ontology/GO_Analysis_Results")

################################################################################
# LOAD DATA
################################################################################

marker_info <- read.csv("marker_information_detailed.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
snp_gene_map <- read.csv("SNP_to_gene_mapping.csv", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

cat("✓ Files loaded!\n")
cat("Marker info rows:", nrow(marker_info), "\n")
cat("SNP mapping rows:", nrow(snp_gene_map), "\n\n")

################################################################################
# FIX COLUMN NAMES (Handle different formats)
################################################################################

# Standardize column names to work with script
# Convert dots to underscores if needed
colnames(marker_info) <- gsub("\\.", "_", colnames(marker_info))
colnames(snp_gene_map) <- gsub("\\.", "_", colnames(snp_gene_map))

cat("Column names standardized\n")
cat("Marker info columns:", paste(colnames(marker_info), collapse = ", "), "\n\n")

################################################################################
# CREATE TABLE
################################################################################

# Get genome-wide significant SNPs
snp_table <- marker_info %>%
  filter(significant == TRUE) %>%
  group_by(marker) %>%
  slice_min(p_value, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  left_join(
    snp_gene_map %>%
      group_by(marker) %>%
      summarize(genes = paste(sort(unique(gene_id)), collapse = ", "), .groups = "drop"),
    by = "marker"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Chr = sprintf("%02d", as.numeric(gsub("^[A-Z]+", "", chromosome))),
    Position = format(position, big.mark = ",", scientific = FALSE),
    Trait = trait_name,
    `P-value` = sprintf("%.2e", p_value),
    Genes = ifelse(is.na(genes) | genes == "", "None (gene desert)", genes),
    Category = ifelse(as.numeric(gsub("BIO_", "", trait_name)) <= 11, "Temperature", "Precipitation")
  ) %>%
  select(SNP = marker, Chr, Position, Trait, `P-value`, Genes, Category) %>%
  arrange(`P-value`)

################################################################################
# DISPLAY TABLE
################################################################################

cat("========================================\n")
cat("GENOME-WIDE SIGNIFICANT SNPs\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
print(snp_table)

################################################################################
# SAVE TABLES
################################################################################

# Save complete table
write.csv(snp_table, "Table_GenomeWide_SNPs.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("\n✓ Saved: Table_GenomeWide_SNPs.csv\n")

# Save as tab-delimited
write.table(snp_table, "Table_GenomeWide_SNPs.txt",
            sep = "\t", row.names = FALSE, quote = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved: Table_GenomeWide_SNPs.txt\n")

# Temperature table
temp_table <- snp_table %>% filter(Category == "Temperature") %>% select(-Category)
write.csv(temp_table, "Table_Temperature_SNPs.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved: Table_Temperature_SNPs.csv\n")

# Precipitation table
precip_table <- snp_table %>% filter(Category == "Precipitation") %>% select(-Category)
write.csv(precip_table, "Table_Precipitation_SNPs.csv", row.names = FALSE)
cat("✓ Saved: Table_Precipitation_SNPs.csv\n")

################################################################################
# PRINT BY CATEGORY
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("TEMPERATURE-ASSOCIATED SNPs\n")
cat("========================================\n")
print(temp_table, row.names = FALSE)

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("PRECIPITATION-ASSOCIATED SNPs\n")
cat("========================================\n")
print(precip_table, row.names = FALSE)

################################################################################
# SUMMARY
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("SUMMARY\n")
cat("========================================\n")
cat("Total SNPs:", nrow(snp_table), "\n")
cat("SNPs with genes:", sum(!grepl("None", snp_table$Genes)), "\n")
cat("Gene deserts:", sum(grepl("None", snp_table$Genes)), "\n")
cat("Unique genes:", 
    length(unique(unlist(strsplit(snp_table$Genes[!grepl("None", snp_table$Genes)], ", ")))), "\n")
cat("Temperature SNPs:", sum(snp_table$Category == "Temperature"), "\n")
cat("Precipitation SNPs:", sum(snp_table$Category == "Precipitation"), "\n")

cat("\n✓✓✓ SUCCESS! Tables created in:\n")
cat(getwd(), "\n")

################################################################################
# END
################################################################################
```

******Supplemental Image**********
```{r}
# Publication-Ready GO Enrichment Figure
# Focuses on Temperature (BIO4) vs Precipitation (BIO15) contrast

################################################################################
# SETUP
################################################################################

library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

# Set working directory
setwd("C:/Users/Lizzy/OneDrive/Documents/UH_Manoa_Classes/IWG_Chapter_2/Analysis_Outputs_and_Visualizations/Gene Ontology/GO_Analysis_Results")

################################################################################
# OPTION 1: Temperature Group vs Precipitation Group (RECOMMENDED)
################################################################################

# Load your GO enrichment results
temp_go <- read.csv("GO_enrichment_temperature_group.csv")
precip_go <- read.csv("GO_enrichment_precipitation_group.csv")

# Select top terms by FDR (most significant)
top_temp <- temp_go %>%
  filter(FDR < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(FDR) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  mutate(
    Category = "Temperature-Associated",
    neg_log10_FDR = -log10(FDR)
  )

top_precip <- precip_go %>%
  filter(FDR < 0.05) %>%
  arrange(FDR) %>%
  head(10) %>%
  mutate(
    Category = "Precipitation-Associated",
    neg_log10_FDR = -log10(FDR)
  )

# Combine
plot_data <- bind_rows(top_temp, top_precip) %>%
  mutate(
    # Clean up term names (remove "GO:XXXXXXX" if present)
    Term_clean = gsub("GO:[0-9]+\\s*", "", Term),
    # Shorten long terms
    Term_clean = ifelse(nchar(Term_clean) > 50, 
                        paste0(substr(Term_clean, 1, 47), "..."), 
                        Term_clean)
  )

# Create figure
p1 <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = neg_log10_FDR, y = reorder(Term_clean, neg_log10_FDR), 
                             fill = Category)) +
  geom_col() +
  facet_wrap(~Category, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Temperature-Associated" = "#D55E00",
                               "Precipitation-Associated" = "#0072B2")) +
  labs(
    x = "-log10(FDR)",
    y = "",
    title = "Enriched Gene Ontology Terms in Climate-Associated Genes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", size = 0.5)

# Save figure
ggsave("Figure_GO_Enrichment_Clean.png", p1, 
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave("Figure_GO_Enrichment_Clean.pdf", p1, 
       width = 10, height = 6, bg = "white")

cat("✓ Saved: Figure_GO_Enrichment_Clean.png\n")
cat("✓ Saved: Figure_GO_Enrichment_Clean.pdf\n")

################################################################################
# OPTION 2: Single Panel with Categories (More Compact)
################################################################################

# Order by category and FDR
plot_data2 <- plot_data %>%
  arrange(Category, FDR) %>%
  mutate(
    Term_ordered = factor(Term_clean, levels = unique(Term_clean))
  )

p2 <- ggplot(plot_data2, aes(x = neg_log10_FDR, y = Term_ordered, fill = Category)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("Temperature-Associated" = "#D55E00",
               "Precipitation-Associated" = "#0072B2"),
    name = ""
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "-log10(FDR)",
    y = "",
    title = "Enriched Gene Ontology Terms"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 11)
  ) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = -log10(0.05), linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", size = 0.5)

ggsave("Figure_GO_Enrichment_Single.png", p2, 
       width = 8, height = 7, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave("Figure_GO_Enrichment_Single.pdf", p2, 
       width = 8, height = 7, bg = "white")

cat("✓ Saved: Figure_GO_Enrichment_Single.png\n")
cat("✓ Saved: Figure_GO_Enrichment_Single.pdf\n")

################################################################################
# OPTION 3: Show Gene Counts (More Informative)
################################################################################

# Add gene ratio for better context
plot_data3 <- bind_rows(top_temp, top_precip) %>%
  mutate(
    Term_clean = gsub("GO:[0-9]+\\s*", "", Term),
    Term_clean = ifelse(nchar(Term_clean) > 45, 
                        paste0(substr(Term_clean, 1, 42), "..."), 
                        Term_clean),
    Gene_ratio = Significant / Annotated,
    Label = sprintf("%d/%d", Significant, Annotated)
  )

p3 <- ggplot(plot_data3, aes(x = neg_log10_FDR, y = reorder(Term_clean, neg_log10_FDR))) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = Category)) +
  geom_text(aes(label = Label), hjust = -0.1, size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~Category, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Temperature-Associated" = "#D55E00",
                               "Precipitation-Associated" = "#0072B2")) +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    x = "-log10(FDR)",
    y = "",
    title = "Enriched Gene Ontology Terms",
    subtitle = "Numbers show candidate genes / total annotated genes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, color = "grey30")
  )

ggsave("Figure_GO_Enrichment_WithCounts.png", p3, 
       width = 11, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave("Figure_GO_Enrichment_WithCounts.pdf", p3, 
       width = 11, height = 6, bg = "white")

cat("✓ Saved: Figure_GO_Enrichment_WithCounts.png\n")
cat("✓ Saved: Figure_GO_Enrichment_WithCounts.pdf\n")

################################################################################
# OPTION 4: Dot Plot (Elegant Alternative)
################################################################################

p4 <- ggplot(plot_data3, aes(x = neg_log10_FDR, y = reorder(Term_clean, neg_log10_FDR))) +
  geom_point(aes(size = Significant, fill = Category), 
             shape = 21, color = "black", stroke = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~Category, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Temperature-Associated" = "#D55E00",
                               "Precipitation-Associated" = "#0072B2")) +
  scale_size_continuous(name = "Gene count", range = c(2, 10)) +
  labs(
    x = "-log10(FDR)",
    y = "",
    title = "Enriched Gene Ontology Terms"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold", size = 11),
    strip.background = element_rect(fill = "grey90", color = NA),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13)
  )

ggsave("Figure_GO_Enrichment_DotPlot.png", p4, 
       width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 600, bg = "white")
ggsave("Figure_GO_Enrichment_DotPlot.pdf", p4, 
       width = 10, height = 6, bg = "white")

cat("✓ Saved: Figure_GO_Enrichment_DotPlot.png\n")
cat("✓ Saved: Figure_GO_Enrichment_DotPlot.pdf\n")

################################################################################
# SUMMARY
################################################################################

cat("\n========================================\n")
cat("CREATED 4 FIGURE OPTIONS:\n")
cat("========================================\n")
cat("1. Figure_GO_Enrichment_Clean.png - Two-panel bar chart (RECOMMENDED)\n")
cat("2. Figure_GO_Enrichment_Single.png - Single panel, color-coded\n")
cat("3. Figure_GO_Enrichment_WithCounts.png - With gene counts labeled\n")
cat("4. Figure_GO_Enrichment_DotPlot.png - Elegant dot plot\n\n")

cat("RECOMMENDATION: Use Option 1 (Clean) or Option 3 (WithCounts)\n")
cat("These clearly show the contrast between hormone signaling (temperature)\n")
cat("and chromatin remodeling (precipitation)\n")

################################################################################
# END
################################################################################
```
